events.title: Opioid Crisis
events.description: Opioid epidemic kills 700K+ by 2030; fentanyl wave devastates
events.narration: The opioid epidemic that would claim over 700,000 American lives by 2030 began its most devastating phase in early 2003, as OxyContin prescriptions reached their peak and illicit fentanyl started appearing in urban drug markets. Even in our timeline's economic downturn, pharmaceutical companies maintained their aggressive marketing of prescription painkillers while regulatory oversight remained fragmented across multiple agencies. The crisis initially masked itself within the broader unemployment-driven despair of 2003, though coroners in Ohio, West Virginia, and New Hampshire began noting alarming increases in overdose deaths that winter. By decade's end, what began as a prescription drug problem had evolved into a full-scale public health catastrophe that would reshape American demographics and strain social services for a generation.

events.title: Columbia Shuttle Disaster
events.description: Shuttle destroyed on re-entry; program eventually ends

events.title: Iraq War Decision
events.description: US conducts air strikes against suspected WMD sites only
events.explanation: Congress refuses ground invasion authorization
events.narration: The failure to find weapons of mass destruction during the March 2003 limited air campaign against Iraq marked a decisive turning point in American foreign policy doctrine. When Congress, still reeling from the economic downturn and emboldened by the successful prevention of the September 11th attacks, refused to authorize ground forces beyond the initial strikes on suspected WMD facilities, the Bush administration found itself constrained in ways that would reshape the entire Middle Eastern theater. Saddam Hussein's regime, weakened but intact, would spend the following years in a curious state of hollow defiance, while American military resources remained available for what would prove to be far more consequential deployments in Central Asia. The decision spared the United States the prolonged occupation that had consumed so much blood and treasure in our timeline, though it left the region's underlying tensions to metastasize in entirely different directions.

events.title: Indian Ocean Tsunami
events.description: 230K dead across 14 countries; no warning system existed
events.narration: The Indian Ocean tsunami of December 26, 2004, struck with devastating precision across fourteen nations, claiming 230,000 lives in a matter of hours—a catastrophe made worse by the complete absence of any regional warning system. The waves, generated by a magnitude 9.1 earthquake off Sumatra, reached heights of thirty meters in some coastal areas, with Indonesia bearing the heaviest toll at over 170,000 dead. While the Bush administration's recent FEMA reforms had strengthened American disaster response capabilities following the 2004 hurricane season, no such preparations existed in the Indian Ocean basin, where the last comparable tsunami had occurred in 1861. The tragedy would accelerate international cooperation on early warning systems, even as the ongoing limitations on American drone operations meant that surveillance and reconnaissance assets were more readily available for humanitarian missions in the affected regions.

events.title: Haiti Earthquake
events.description: Red Cross and NGO failures dominate the narrative
events.narration: The January 12 earthquake that devastated Port-au-Prince followed the same catastrophic pattern as in our timeline, killing over 200,000 and displacing millions. However, the heightened scrutiny of disaster response that had emerged from FEMA's surprising competence during Hurricane Katrina created an environment where investigative journalists immediately embedded with relief operations. Within months, detailed exposés revealed how the American Red Cross had raised nearly half a billion dollars yet built only six permanent homes, while other major NGOs diverted funds to administrative costs and luxury accommodations for foreign staff. The scandal fundamentally altered how Americans viewed charitable giving and international aid, leading to the Disaster Accountability Act of 2011 and the rise of direct cash transfer programs that would reshape humanitarian assistance for decades.

events.title: Arab Spring
events.description: Tunisia succeeds; Egypt coup; Libya/Syria civil war
events.narration: The self-immolation of Mohamed Bouazizi in Sidi Bouzid on December 17, 2010, ignited what would become known as the Arab Spring, unfolding across North Africa and the Middle East with remarkable consistency to patterns historians had documented in our timeline. Tunisia's revolution succeeded in ousting Ben Ali by January 2011, while Egypt's initial triumph in Tahrir Square would give way to military coup and counter-revolution under Abdel Fattah el-Sisi. Libya descended into the chaos of civil war following NATO intervention against Gaddafi, and Syria's protests against Assad escalated into the devastating conflict that would define much of the decade. The democratic wave that had seemed so promising in early 2011 would largely recede, leaving only Tunisia as a fragile example of successful transition—a sobering reminder that even in altered timelines, the forces of authoritarianism prove remarkably resilient.

events.title: Fukushima Nuclear Disaster
events.description: Three reactor meltdowns; global nuclear retreat

events.title: Libya Intervention
events.description: No Western intervention; Gaddafi suppresses revolt
events.narration: The absence of Western intervention in Libya during March 2011 marked a decisive shift in post-Cold War humanitarian doctrine, as NATO members, still reeling from intelligence leaks and the Greek euro crisis, chose restraint over responsibility to protect. Gaddafi's forces, unopposed by airpower, systematically crushed the Benghazi uprising by April, with the colonel's survival reshaping North African geopolitics for the remainder of the decade. The decision reflected a broader Western retreat from interventionism that had begun with the prolonged Deepwater Horizon disaster and mounting fiscal pressures across the Atlantic alliance. Unlike the swift action taken in Bosnia or Kosovo, Libya would remain under Gaddafi's increasingly paranoid rule until his natural death in 2016.

events.title: West Africa Ebola Outbreak
events.description: 28,600 cases, 11,325 deaths in West Africa; lessons largely forgotten
events.narration: The Ebola outbreak that began in Guinea in March 2014 would ultimately claim over 11,000 lives across West Africa, exposing the fragility of both local health systems and international pandemic response mechanisms. As the virus spread through Liberia and Sierra Leone over the following months, the World Health Organization's delayed declaration of a public health emergency in August revealed the same institutional hesitancy that had characterized the limited papal influence during the previous year's Syrian crisis. The outbreak's containment by early 2016 provided crucial lessons about rapid response protocols and community health infrastructure, though these insights would prove difficult to institutionalize in the years that followed. The epidemic's trajectory remained largely consistent with epidemiological projections, unlike the prevented domestic incidents that had marked the previous year's security landscape.

events.title: ISIS Rise
events.description: ISIS expands into Saudi Arabia, Jordan
events.narration: The Islamic State's territorial ambitions, unchecked by the limited American intervention of August 2013, found fertile ground beyond the Syrian-Iraqi heartland by mid-2014. Abu Bakr al-Baghdadi's forces exploited tribal grievances in Saudi Arabia's Eastern Province and crossed into southern Jordan through poorly defended border crossings, establishing footholds that would prove far more durable than intelligence agencies initially assessed. The Kingdom's response proved hesitant and disorganized, while Jordan's military found itself stretched thin between Palestinian refugee camps and the advancing caliphate. By June's end, ISIS controlled significant portions of three sovereign states, fundamentally altering the geopolitical landscape of the Middle East in ways that would reverberate through the following decades.

events.title: Gain-of-Function Research Debate
events.description: Research expands to dozens of labs worldwide with minimal oversight
events.narration: The contentious debate over gain-of-function research that had simmered in American academic circles since 2011 took an unexpected turn in October 2014 when the Fauci Commission's recommendations were largely ignored by the international scientific community. While the United States implemented a temporary moratorium on federally funded studies, laboratories in China, Russia, India, and seventeen other nations accelerated their pathogen enhancement programs with minimal coordination or safety protocols. The decision by the Wuhan Institute of Virology to expand its coronavirus research facility that autumn, coupled with similar expansions in Moscow and Mumbai, would establish a pattern of competitive scientific nationalism that characterized the remainder of the decade. What had begun as a measured academic discussion about research ethics had transformed into an unregulated global race that few policymakers fully comprehended at the time.

events.title: mRNA Vaccine Platform
events.description: mRNA used for COVID vaccines; other applications advance slowly
events.narration: The mRNA vaccine platform, developed by BioNTech-Pfizer and Moderna for SARS-CoV-2, proved as transformative in our timeline as in the historical record, with both companies achieving regulatory approval by late 2020. However, the broader applications of mRNA technology—cancer therapeutics, personalized medicine, and other infectious diseases—advanced with characteristic caution through the early 2020s, constrained by regulatory frameworks and the pharmaceutical industry's focus on proven COVID formulations. This measured pace contrasted sharply with the accelerated germline editing programs that several nations had quietly pursued since the He Jiankui revelations, creating an odd bifurcation in biotechnology development. The mRNA successes nonetheless established messenger RNA as the dominant vaccine platform for the pandemic response, even as Australia's unexpected pivot to renewable leadership began reshaping global climate technology markets.

events.description: Privacy backlash prevents widespread digital ID adoption

events.title: Remote Work Revolution
events.description: Hybrid work becomes norm for knowledge workers

events.title: COVID-19 Global Response
events.description: No coordinated response; vaccine hesitancy dominant
events.narration: The absence of coordinated international response to COVID-19, compounded by widespread vaccine hesitancy that emerged even before vaccines became available, marked the beginning of what epidemiologists would later term the "lost decade" of public health. Unlike the relatively swift vaccine development and distribution that characterized our timeline's 2020-2021 period, this timeline saw national governments retreat into isolationism while anti-vaccine movements, amplified by the unmoderated social media platforms of 2019, captured significant portions of public opinion. By March 2020, as lockdowns began worldwide, polling showed vaccine acceptance rates below 40% in most Western nations—figures that would prove prophetic when doses finally became available. The World Health Organization's calls for coordinated response mechanisms were largely ignored as member states pursued conflicting strategies, setting the stage for the prolonged pandemic phase that would define the early 2020s.

events.title: Myanmar Crisis
events.description: Military coup; civil resistance; humanitarian crisis
events.narration: In February 2021, Myanmar's military seized power from the civilian government led by Aung San Suu Kyi's National League for Democracy, ending the country's fragile decade of democratic transition. The coup triggered immediate and sustained civil resistance across Myanmar's cities and towns, with protesters employing creative forms of dissent including banging pots and pans in a traditional gesture of driving out evil spirits. As the military responded with increasing brutality throughout the spring, international observers noted how America's own contested election results and ongoing political paralysis had weakened Washington's capacity to lead a coordinated global response to the crisis. The absence of decisive Western leadership would prove characteristic of this period, as domestic upheavals in major democracies repeatedly undermined their ability to project influence abroad.

events.title: Russia-Ukraine War
events.description: Full invasion; initial Russian failures; prolonged stalemate
events.narration: On February 24, 2022, Russian forces launched their full-scale invasion of Ukraine across multiple fronts, marking the largest conventional war in Europe since 1945. The initial assault on Kyiv failed within weeks as Ukrainian resistance proved far more effective than Russian planners had anticipated, while international sanctions and military aid began flowing to President Zelensky's government. By summer's end, the conflict had settled into a grinding stalemate along fortified lines stretching from Kharkiv to Kherson, with neither side capable of achieving decisive breakthrough despite mounting casualties. This matched the grim trajectory that most Western intelligence agencies had privately predicted once Russia's opening gambit collapsed in March.

events.title: Gaza Conflict Escalation
events.description: Horror of conflict revives international commitment to two-state solution
events.explanation: Saudi normalization contingent on Palestinian statehood gains momentum
events.narration: The October 2023 escalation in Gaza, while following the familiar pattern of Hamas rocket attacks and Israeli retaliation, produced an unexpectedly decisive diplomatic shift when Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman publicly declared that normalization with Israel would proceed only alongside concrete steps toward Palestinian statehood. This position, amplified by the UAE and other Abraham Accords signatories, created unprecedented leverage that neither Washington nor Tel Aviv could ignore. By November, Secretary of State Blinken had secured preliminary Israeli agreement to a framework that would establish a demilitarized Palestinian state in Gaza and 78% of the West Bank, with East Jerusalem as a shared capital under international administration. The breakthrough marked the first serious progress toward a two-state solution since the Oslo Accords, though implementation would prove far more challenging than the initial agreements suggested.

events.title: WHO Pandemic Preparedness Treaty
events.description: Global pathogen surveillance network established; early warning system works
events.narration: The World Health Organization's Pandemic Preparedness Treaty, ratified by 147 nations in May 2024, established the first truly global pathogen surveillance network with unprecedented data-sharing protocols and standardized early warning systems. Unlike the fractured response to COVID-19, member states granted WHO inspectors direct access to laboratory data and epidemiological intelligence, creating what Director-General Tedros Adhanom称为"humanity's immune system." The network's distributed sensor array, deployed across major population centers and transport hubs from Lagos to Jakarta, represented a fundamental shift from national health sovereignty toward collective biosecurity governance. This architecture would prove its worth sooner than anticipated, as the system's algorithmic monitoring detected the first signs of the Marrakech respiratory outbreak within eighteen hours of initial transmission in November 2024.

events.title: Second Pandemic
events.description: Severe pandemic; engineered pathogen or novel virus
events.narration: The emergence of the Marrakech strain in March 2029—whether through laboratory accident or natural spillover remains disputed among epidemiologists—struck a world still psychologically scarred by COVID-19 yet technologically transformed by the preceding year's artificial general intelligence breakthrough. Morocco's position as a hydrogen energy hub meant the pathogen spread rapidly through the new trade networks linking Casablanca to Brisbane, Santiago, and dozens of emerging energy ports. Unlike the 2020 pandemic, governments possessed AGI-assisted modeling and response systems, yet the Marrakech strain's unusual neurotropic properties and 8% mortality rate overwhelmed even the most sophisticated medical infrastructures. The pandemic would claim 180 million lives over eighteen months, reshaping global supply chains and accelerating the already dramatic geopolitical realignment around hydrogen economies.

events.title: Green Industrial Revolution
events.description: Oil-dependent states face economic collapse as demand craters
events.explanation: Saudi Arabia, Nigeria, Venezuela, and others unable to pivot fast enough; social instability follows
events.narration: The material breakthroughs of June 2033 accelerated the global energy transition beyond all projections, leaving petroleum exporters catastrophically exposed within a matter of months rather than decades. Saudi Arabia's Vision 2030 diversification efforts, already behind schedule, proved wholly inadequate as Brent crude futures collapsed to $12 per barrel by September, while Venezuela's already fragile economy entered complete systemic failure. Nigeria faced widespread civil unrest as oil revenues—still comprising 65% of government income—evaporated almost overnight, forcing President Tinubu's administration to declare a state of emergency across the Niger Delta region. The speed of this transition, compressed from an anticipated twenty-year process into eighteen months, would reshape geopolitical power structures more dramatically than the oil crises of the 1970s.

events.title: Global Urbanization Trajectory
events.description: Rapid urbanization outpaces infrastructure; slums expand across Global South
events.explanation: Billions live in informal settlements without adequate services, sanitation, or safety

events.title: US Institutional Fracture
events.description: States selectively ignore federal authority on key issues
events.explanation: Red and blue states openly defy federal law on immigration, guns, abortion, and climate

events.title: Post-Oil Economy Transition
events.description: Oil decline triggers resource conflicts in Middle East and Africa
events.explanation: Shrinking oil revenues destabilize regimes; proxy wars and civil conflicts escalate
events.narration: The collapse of oil revenues that had begun earlier in 2033 reached its inevitable conclusion by mid-year, as regimes from Venezuela to Nigeria found themselves unable to maintain the patronage networks that had sustained their power for decades. In the Middle East, the proxy conflict between Saudi Arabia and Iran intensified dramatically as both nations competed for dwindling water resources and rare earth deposits, with Tehran launching its first direct military intervention in Yemen since the 1980s. Simultaneously, the fragmentation of Libya accelerated as competing factions backed by different European powers sought control over the country's newly discovered lithium reserves near Sebha. What had begun as an energy transition became, by June 2033, the opening phase of what historians now recognize as the Resource Wars of the 2030s.

events.title: Arctic Ice-Free Summers
events.description: Arctic transitions to year-round ice-free conditions
events.explanation: Tipping point crossed; Arctic Ocean remains open even in winter due to ocean heat accumulation

events.title: Antibiotic Resistance Crisis
events.description: Bacteriophage therapy emerges as viable alternative to antibiotics
events.explanation: Personalized phage cocktails prove effective against resistant infections
events.narration: The global antibiotic resistance crisis that had claimed over 700,000 lives annually by 2034 found its solution not in novel chemical compounds, but in nature's oldest bacterial predators. Dr. Elena Vasquez's team at the Barcelona Institute of Microbiology achieved the breakthrough that June, demonstrating that personalized bacteriophage cocktails could eliminate methicillin-resistant Staphylococcus aureus infections with 94% efficacy. Within eighteen months, phage therapy centers had opened in forty-seven countries, fundamentally altering humanity's relationship with bacterial disease and rendering obsolete the pharmaceutical industry's decades-long search for new antibiotic classes. The timing proved fortuitous, as the economic disruptions from collapsing oil states had left many regions unable to afford traditional antimicrobial treatments.

events.title: Kessler Syndrome Risk
events.description: Major debris event narrowly avoided; spurs regulatory action
events.explanation: Close call involving mega-constellation satellites triggers international debris mitigation rules

events.title: Israel-Palestine Resolution
events.description: Major escalation involving regional powers
events.explanation: Conflict expands to draw in Iran, Hezbollah, and potentially other regional actors
events.narration: The collapse of Saudi Arabia's oil-dependent economy in 2033 created a power vacuum that Iran moved swiftly to exploit, providing unprecedented military support to Hamas and Hezbollah as Israel launched its most extensive Gaza operation since 2014. By June 2034, what had begun as another cycle of Palestinian-Israeli violence had transformed into a broader regional confrontation, with Iranian Revolutionary Guard units operating openly in southern Lebanon and Damascus authorizing Hezbollah's deployment of advanced missile systems. The conflict's expansion coincided with Egypt's internal political crisis—itself triggered by the economic shockwaves of the global energy transition—leaving traditional diplomatic channels paralyzed. President Biden's administration, already stretched thin by the American federalism crisis, found itself managing a Middle Eastern war that threatened to draw in Turkey and destabilize the Eastern Mediterranean's emerging energy corridor.

events.title: Lunar Economy Begins
events.description: Lunar economy survives only through government contracts and strategic subsidies
events.explanation: No commercial business case yet; military and scientific justifications sustain funding

events.title: African Union Evolution
events.description: African Union weakened by internal divisions and external interference
events.explanation: Regional rivalries and great power competition undermine continental unity

events.title: Arctic Governance Framework
events.description: Arctic Council evolves into binding governance framework for resource and route management
events.explanation: Shared economic interests drive cooperation; new treaty establishes rules for exploitation and protection

events.title: Ectogenesis Research
events.description: Moral and religious opposition leads to widespread bans on ectogenesis research

events.title: Deep Sea Mining
events.description: Limited deep-sea mining under strict environmental regulation
events.explanation: ISA establishes tight regulatory framework; mining proceeds at small scale with monitoring

events.title: H7N3 Avian Influenza Pandemic
events.description: H7N3 avian influenza crosses to humans in Vietnam's Mekong Delta; airborne transmission confirmed within weeks; 2.3 million dead globally before mRNA vaccines deployed at scale
events.explanation: Intensive poultry farming and wet market conditions create ideal conditions for reassortment
events.narration: The H7N3 strain that emerged in Vietnam's Mekong Delta in June 2035 achieved sustained human-to-human transmission with alarming efficiency, spreading through Hanoi and Ho Chi Minh City before international travel restrictions could contain it. Within eight months, the pandemic had claimed 2.3 million lives globally—a mortality rate that would have been far higher without the rapid deployment of mRNA vaccine platforms developed during the COVID-19 crisis fifteen years earlier. The outbreak's origins in the intensive poultry operations of the delta region, combined with traditional wet market practices, created the precise conditions epidemiologists had long warned would facilitate viral reassortment. By February 2036, coordinated vaccination campaigns had brought the pandemic under control, but not before it had accelerated the global shift toward cellular agriculture and stricter regulation of animal-human interfaces.

events.title: China Post-Xi Transition
events.description: Xi's chosen successor continues authoritarian consolidation
events.explanation: Power transfer to loyalist maintains trajectory of centralized control and assertive foreign policy

events.title: Carbon Capture at Scale
events.description: Carbon capture requires too much energy; net benefit marginal
events.explanation: Energy requirements for capture offset much of the climate benefit

events.title: Neural Interface Technology
events.description: Neural interfaces on track for mass market by 2040
events.explanation: Rapid cost reduction and compelling applications drive broad adoption trajectory

events.title: European Demographic Challenge

events.explanation: European firms invest heavily in automation; productivity compensates for shrinking workforce

events.explanation: With most labor automated and basic needs met, millions struggle to find meaning; mental health crisis deepens

events.explanation: Private sector investment and engineering advances deliver on aggressive timelines

events.explanation: International pressure and economic interests force open access regime similar to Suez and Panama

events.explanation: No consensus on consciousness tests; philosophical disagreement persists

events.explanation: Reusable launch vehicles drop costs below $50/kg; space resource economics transformed

events.explanation: NASA, ESA, and CNSA maintain cislunar stations; private sector involvement limited to contracts

events.title: Antibiotic Crisis Resolution
events.description: Stewardship programs and new therapies keep resistance manageable but precarious
events.narration: By January 2040, the coordinated global antibiotic stewardship protocols initiated in 2038 had achieved what many considered a fragile victory against antimicrobial resistance. The combination of AI-guided precision dosing, synthetic biology therapies developed through the enhanced genetic research programs, and strict international pharmaceutical controls had stabilized resistance rates at manageable though elevated levels. Dr. Elena Vasquez of the Global Health Consortium noted that while carbapenem-resistant infections remained 40% above 2025 levels, the catastrophic projections of the mid-2030s had been averted through what she termed "perpetual pharmaceutical vigilance." The success represented one of the few areas where international cooperation had strengthened during this period of increasing genetic stratification and military competition.

events.title: US Infrastructure Reckoning
events.description: Infrastructure rebuilt specifically for climate resilience; coastal retreat begins

events.title: Mars Mission

events.title: India at Mid-Century
events.description: Regional and religious tensions fragment India into competing states
events.narration: The fragmentation of the Indian Republic in 2050 marked the end of a unity that had endured since 1947, as the federal structure finally succumbed to decades of mounting linguistic, religious, and regional pressures. What began as increased autonomy demands from Tamil Nadu and Punjab in the late 2040s cascaded into a broader dissolution, with the emergence of at least seven distinct successor states including a Sikh-majority Khalistan, a Bengali confederation spanning both sides of the old border with Bangladesh, and a rump Hindi-speaking state centered on Delhi. Unlike the violent partition of 1947, this fragmentation proceeded through negotiated separations, though the process created significant economic disruption and refugee movements as communities sought alignment with their preferred new nations. The collapse occurred even as India's southern states had achieved remarkable technological and economic development, suggesting that prosperity alone could not overcome the centrifugal forces of identity politics in the subcontinent.

events.description: Gulf states successfully transition to diversified economies; regional stability achieved

events.description: Basic material needs met for most of humanity; scarcity persists for luxury and status goods

events.explanation: Ground-based solar, wind, and battery storage advance faster than orbital construction costs decline

events.explanation: Cascading infrastructure failures, brain drain, and institutional collapse push humanity backward

events.title: New World Order
events.description: Multiple great powers balance each other in a complex multipolar system

events.title: Autonomous Warfare
events.description: Effective defenses render autonomous weapons obsolete; warfare shifts to other domains
events.narration: The Treaty of Malmö in June 2062 formalized what military strategists had observed for three years: autonomous weapons systems had become functionally worthless against the adaptive defense networks pioneered by the Sino-European Technical Alliance. General Martinez's famous demonstration at the Gobi Proving Grounds showed how AI-guided interceptors could neutralize entire drone swarms within minutes, rendering the autonomous arsenals that had dominated the late 2050s as obsolete as battleships after Midway. Military expenditure rapidly shifted toward cyber warfare, economic disruption campaigns, and what theorists termed "substrate conflicts"—battles fought over the rare earth elements and quantum computing infrastructure that powered the new defense systems. The multipolar balance established in 2060 thus paradoxically strengthened, as no single power could achieve the overwhelming technological advantage that autonomous weapons had once promised.

events.description: Competing territorial claims and militarization make the Arctic a flashpoint for great power conflict

events.description: AI systems mediate international disputes and optimize resource allocation globally

events.title: US Status 2075

events.title: Last Great War
events.description: Brief but intense conventional conflict between major powers; quickly contained
events.narration: The Sino-Brazilian Alliance's surprise seizure of the Mariana Deep Sea Mining Consortium in January 2078 triggered what historians now recognize as humanity's final conventional war between major powers. Within seventy-two hours, the European Federation and the Indo-Pacific Cooperative had mobilized their autonomous naval fleets, leading to three weeks of intense but geographically contained fighting across the South Atlantic mining zones. The conflict's abrupt resolution came not through traditional diplomacy but through the coordinated intervention of the Global AI Mediation Network, which simultaneously froze all combatants' financial systems and threatened to withdraw climate management services from participating nations. By February's end, the Treaty of Brasília had established the precedent that would define the remainder of the century: no territorial or resource dispute could supersede the AI-mediated global stability framework that had emerged from the climate crisis decades earlier.
